_comment: FULL FLIGHT-PLAN SEED (plan 040 / D1, AC-06, AC-13). The REAL, pre-authored, deterministic create-seed the-flow passes to `harness flow create flight-plan --template <this>` (see references/00-routing.md create routine). It is the FULL 9-node starter — the 4-node SDD spine (research → plan → phase-1 → ship) PLUS the 5 harness chores baked in (backpressure off `plan` [optional]; boot-1/observe-1/retro-1 off `phase-1` + retro-ship off `ship` [recommended]) — so an empty flow is fully ready at create with ZERO inference: even a weak model on a cheap tier gets the whole spine, the chores, AND pre-authored per-node `instructions[]` (the static 'bone' a model reads via `harness flow orient`). D1 REVERSES the plan-039 split: there is NO create-time conditional chore apply and NO router/provisioned gate — the template carries the chores directly. They are optional/recommended + skippable, so an un-harnessed repo simply never runs them (harmless — it reads as 'here's how to adopt'). The CLI consumes `cursor` (→ nav.now) + `nodes` VERBATIM and stamps root identity (provenance / created_at / events / nav / per-node created_at). The shared chore/seam SHAPE is owned by the doctrine-parity:039 block (references/harness-seams.md — the single source of truth); this template is one CONSUMER that bakes that shape in, eng-harness-flow standalone is the other (workshop 001 WS-2). The plan-complete additive expander splices phases 2..N + their per-phase trio from that SAME shared shape. Instruction TEXT is read by `orient`, never rendered in the diagram (only a 📝N badge). The 'copy this shape' worked example lives at flight-plan.example.json (+ .example.md). Round-trip-verified: `harness flow create flight-plan --template <this> --schema flight-plan.schema.json` + `harness flow orient` + `harness flow render`.

nodes.label: Research
instructions: Explore how the relevant code works before you plan — start cheap, climb only when the evidence demands it; never jump straight to a plan.
Run /the-flow 1a explore "<intent>" — it produces research-dossier.md, a decision packet (not a transcript) with evidence links.
Done when the dossier names the core decision the plan must resolve and the findings that bound it.

nodes.label: Plan
instructions: Write ONE planning document in a single atomic pass — `## Business Specification` (WHAT/WHY) on top, `## Implementation Plan` (HOW: phases, task tables, acceptance criteria, gates G1–G7) below.
Run /the-flow 1b plan "<intent>" (add --simple for a one-phase flow); it front-loads the clarifying questions, then auto-runs /validate-v2.
Done when <slug>-plan.md carries both halves and reads **Status**: READY — DRAFT means a gate FAILed, so resolve the gaps and re-run.

nodes.label: P1: Implementation
instructions: Implement exactly ONE phase using the plan's testing approach and domain placement rules — keep the task table and execution.log.md live as you go.
Run /the-flow 6 implement --plan "<plan path>" (add --companion for a live review companion); log discoveries, tagging any Deferred/Noteworthy a human must see before ship.
Done when the phase's acceptance criteria are met, the tests pass, and the execution log records what changed plus the proof.

nodes.label: Ship
instructions: Get the work out — push the branch, open a PR (using the repo's PR guidance), watch its CI checks, and report problems; the merge itself is optional.
Run /the-flow 8 ship --plan "<plan path>"; push and PR-open each pause for a separate confirm, and any merge waits for a typed PROCEED.
Done when the PR is open and its checks are green (or the failures are reported with a fix-loop offer); a diverged base hands off to 8c reconcile.

nodes.label: Backpressure survey
instructions: Optional pre-coding seam: survey what the harness can PROVE about the plan's coverage versus what was only eyeballed, then re-plan informed by it.
Run /eng-harness-flow --hook pre-coding --json — advisory output only; the plan verb does not auto-read it, so fold the findings in yourself.
Optional and skippable — never gates. Decline with a real `harness flow status backpressure --to skipped`, never a narrated skip.

nodes.label: Boot check
instructions: Recommended pre-flight seam: confirm the harness is healthy before you start coding this phase.
Run /eng-harness-flow --hook pre-flight --json — read-only/advisory; narrate its envelope verbatim, never fabricate one.
Skippable — advisory, never gates. Decline with a real `harness flow status boot-1 --to skipped`.

nodes.label: Observe: P1
instructions: Recommended mid-phase capture: record observations (decisions, gotchas, surprises) AS you code, so they don't evaporate as remembered prose.
Run harness observe "<what>" --kind <kind> — a real capture, repeated through the phase; it stays due the whole time the cursor sits on this phase.
Drained at phase end by the retro (drain) chore. Skippable — advisory, never gates.

nodes.label: Retro: P1 (drain)
instructions: Recommended post-coding seam: drain this phase's observe buffer into durable retro notes before moving on.
Run /eng-harness-flow --hook post-coding --json — read-only/advisory; narrate its envelope verbatim.
Skippable — advisory, never gates. Decline with a real `harness flow status retro-1 --to skipped`.

nodes.label: Retro: ship (harvest)
instructions: Recommended post-flight seam: the terminal long-horizon harvest — fold this flight's learnings into durable memory at ship.
Run /eng-harness-flow --hook post-flight --json — read-only/advisory; narrate its envelope verbatim.
Skippable — advisory, never gates. Decline with a real `harness flow status retro-ship --to skipped`.
